Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that excludes certain cases based on specific criteria The sequence is Approve Purchase Requisition followed by Create Order followed by Confirm Order and the time between Create Order and Confirm Order must be more than 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the first thing I need to do is identify the cases that meet these two conditions the exact activity sequence and the time difference Once I have those cases I need to exclude all events from those cases in the final result

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: To check for consecutive events I can use window functions Maybe using LAG or LEAD to look at the next event in the same case Alternatively I can assign a row number to each event within a case ordered by timestamp and then check if three consecutive rows have the required activities

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me outline the steps For each case order events by timestamp Check if there is a trio where the first activity is Approve Purchase Requisition the next is Create Order and the next is Confirm Order For those cases calculate the time difference between Create Order and Confirm Order events in that sequence If the difference is more than 5 days add the case to a list of cases to exclude Finally select all events not in that list

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me think of using the LEAD function For each event I can get the next event activity and the next next event activity Then for a row where the current activity is Approve Purchase Requisition the next activity is Create Order and the next next is Confirm Order that is a possible case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait using LEAD would give the immediate next event right Because LEAD gets the row after the current one So if I use LEAD 1 and LEAD 2 then for each event the next event and the one after that So if the current event is Approve Purchase Requisition and the next two are Create Order and Confirm Order then that is the sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The Create Order is the next event so its timestamp is the lead one timestamp The Confirm Order is the next next so lead two timestamp The time between Create Order and Confirm Order needs to be more than 5 days So the difference between their timestamps should be more than 5 days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The subquery would select the case id where such a triplet exists and the time difference meets the condition Once we have those case ids the main query would select all events not in that list

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Using LEAD 1 and LEAD 2 ensures that the next two events are exactly the next ones so there is no other activity in between Because LEAD 1 gets the immediately next row and LEAD 2 gets the one after that So if those two are the required activities then there are no other activities in between

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The case could have multiple instances of the sequence But we just need to exclude the entire case if any occurrence meets the criteria So using distinct case id in the subquery is correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The query should work Let me double check the requirements The sequence must be three in direct succession yes Time between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days yes Exclude all events from such cases yes So this should meet the criteria
